items.title: Have the Chase Sapphire Preferred? You're likely eligible for the Sapphire Reserve. Here's how
items.summary: If you currently have the Chase Sapphire Preferred, you're still eligible for the Chase Sapphire Reserve's high welcome bonus and luxury perks. What to know.

items.title: Last Chance: Best-Ever Chase Sapphire Reserve Card 150K Bonus Points Offer
items.summary: The Chase Sapphire Reserve Card is one of the most popular premium travel credit cards, given its lucrative bonus categories, valuable lounge access, and other credits and benefits that can help offset the annual fee.

items.title: Last chance: Earn Chase Sapphire Reserve's biggest-ever bonus
items.summary: The best Chase Sapphire Reserve welcome offer we've seen in years is ending June 15. Here's why you should get the card now.

items.title: Chase Sapphire Reserve 150,000-point bonus: Your frequent questions answered
items.summary: If you are interested in the Chase Sapphire Reserve and its valuable 150,000-point bonus, you may have questions. Here are answers and everything else to know.

items.title: Marriott Bonvoy Bold Credit Card review: Silver Elite status for no annual fee
items.summary: Marriott offers a no-annual-fee card option for beginner travelers who are loyal to the brand. Let's dive into everything this card has to offer.

items.title: Marriott Bonvoy Boundless Credit Card review: Worth keeping year after year
items.summary: Even if you aren’t a Marriott loyalist, the Marriott Bonvoy Boundless is worth considering. It offers solid perks for a low annual fee. Here's what to know.

items.title: Chase Sapphire Reserve 150,000-Point Bonus Enters Final Week Before June 15 Deadline
items.summary: Chase Sapphire Reserve’s 150,000-point initial bonus ends June 15, with strong travel and dining earning, Sapphire Lounge access, and credits that can outweigh the fee.

items.title: Guide to Ultra-Premium Credit Card Travel Protections
items.summary: Ultra-premium credit cards, such as the Chase Sapphire Reserve and American Express Platinum Card®, have annual fees that commonly exceed $500/year. items.title: Amex to change the name of the Green card. Are changes coming?
items.summary: Last week, Doctor of Credit pointed out that American Express is showing a notice for Amex Green Card holders saying that the name of their card will change on August 20th, 2026, to become the “American Express Classic Green Card”.
